reasoning: The ECB is currently undergoing quantitative tightening, with the balance sheet shrinking predictably due to the unwinding of the Asset Purchase Programme (APP) and Pandemic Emergency Purchase Programme (PEPP), alongside TLTRO repayments. An expected decline of €500 billion over 2025 translates to an average decrease of approximately €10 billion per week. This strong downward drift implies that the total central bank assets are more likely to decrease than increase over any given period. For the shorter dates (1 week and 1 month), the steady QT run-off will likely dominate random weekly noise (such as MRO fluctuations), making an increase unlikely. For the longer horizon (3 months to January 2026), uncertainty is legitimately higher due to a scheduled quarterly revaluation of gold and foreign exchange reserves in early January, which previously injected €156.1 billion into the balance sheet in October 2025. Per instructions to ensure uncertainty increases over the forecast horizon, probabilities for dates further out revert closer to 0.5.
batch_queries: Will the amount of money representing all central bank assets for the euro area have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-10-26?
